text.title: Communications Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the communications-management methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Channel MUST match segment preference (exec → 1-page summary; dev → channel post; ops → dashboard); no one-size-fits-all.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Status reports MUST ship on the agreed cadence; missed report is itself a status signal.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Every meeting action MUST have owner + due date + linked issue before the meeting closes.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Plan MUST include at least one two-way channel per segment (not broadcast-only).
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Comms plan MUST name the crisis-path (who notifies whom in <1h on critical event).
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip when stakeholder count <3 with verbal sync.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
